description_long: This object is a flintlock-style rifle or pistol, featuring a long, slender barrel and a curved wooden grip with a distinct orange-brown coloration and visible wood grain pattern. The barrel and lock mechanism are metallic, showing a dark, weathered finish with some metallic highlights, suggesting age or use. The lock mechanism includes a hammer and frizzen, typical of flintlock firearms, and the trigger guard is a simple metal loop. The muzzle is open and slightly flared, and the overall design is reminiscent of 18th or early 19th-century firearms. There are no visible modern attachments such as scopes or rails, and the magazine is not externally visible, consistent with single-shot, muzzle-loading firearms. The object appears well-crafted, with detailed texturing on both the wood and metal components.
description: A detailed, antique flintlock rifle or pistol with a long metal barrel and a curved, orange-brown wooden grip, featuring classic lock and trigger mechanisms.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, the curved wooden grip, and the lock mechanism.
description_view_1: Front-on view down the barrel, highlighting the open muzzle and the alignment of the barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden grip and the lock mechanism from the other side.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the length of the barrel and the curvature of the grip.
